title: PressureEvent
description: Canonical defensive-pressure event normalized into Campos pitch space. `counterpress` marks pressure applied within 5 seconds of losing possession, per StatsBomb's definition.

matchId.description: Match identifier in the consuming app or source dataset.

teamId.description: Identifier for the team applying pressure.

playerName.description: Display-ready player name when present in the source feed.

addedMinute.description: Stoppage-time minute offset, null when not in added time.

period.description: Match period: 1-2 regular halves, 3-4 extra time, 5 penalty shootout.

x.description: Campos pitch x-coordinate (0 = own goal line, 100 = opposition goal line). Null for events without coordinates.

y.description: Campos pitch y-coordinate (0 = attacker's right, 100 = attacker's left). Null for events without coordinates.

counterpress.description: True when the pressure was applied within 5 seconds of losing possession (StatsBomb's counterpress definition). False for regular open-play pressures. Null when the source provider does not classify counterpress (for example Opta). Note: pressure coordinates are in the PRESSING team's attacker-perspective frame, so `teamId` always identifies the pressing team — consumers rendering from the in-possession team's perspective must flip axes.

provider.description: Source provider identifier such as opta, statsbomb, or whoscored.

providerEventId.description: Raw event identifier from the source provider.

sourceMeta.description: Provider-scoped metadata bag for provenance and advanced debugging. It must stay JSON-serializable, must not duplicate canonical top-level fields, and must not reinterpret raw provider semantics into fake cross-provider meaning.
